topTargets.name: KC Concepcion
topTargets.reasoning: Strongest board convergence available at 1.06: Consensus #8 Tier 2, RSP #5 DoT 86.3 (Starter grade), MFL ADP 13.57. Lands in CLE with real target share runway. Medium RSP affinity rewards a player both Waldman and Consensus love, and our 50/25/25 weighting puts him squarely in range when the four Tier-1s and likely Sadiq/Mendoza are gone.

topTargets.name: Jadarian Price
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #7 Tier 2 (RB2 overall, SEA) — best 'positional value' fallback at 1.06 if Concepcion is gone. RSP #21 DoT 83.5 is tepid (Waldman flags him as Love's sidekick), but our weighting still has him as a justifiable Tier 2 pick. RB room is already 6 deep, so this is contingency rather than preferred.

topTargets.name: Eli Stowers
topTargets.reasoning: Realistic 1.17 target. Consensus #21 Tier 3, RSP #11 DoT 84.4 (TE2 by Waldman, on cusp of Starter), MFL ADP 21.25 — all three boards align in this range. Roster has only 2 TEs, lands in PHI with target tree opening up. Medium RSP affinity gives the Waldman bump just enough lift to take him over a similarly-ranked WR.

topTargets.name: Malachi Fields
topTargets.reasoning: The 2.06 dream scenario. Consensus #22 Tier 3 (WR12, NYG) but RSP #4 DoT 87.2 (Starter, Nico Collins comp) — massive RSP-vs-Consensus arbitrage that our 25% RSP weight rewards. If he slides to 2.06 it's an easy click; even at fair value he is the highest-ceiling WR likely available there.

topTargets.name: Antonio Williams
topTargets.reasoning: Round 3 / late 2.06 fallback. Consensus #24 Tier 4, RSP #18 DoT 83.9 (Jayden Reed comp), MFL ADP 29.06. Lands in WAS with snap path. Cheap rookie contract fits cap-strapped posture, and Waldman bump justifies him over straight ADP names.

capPosture: Cap-tight at $42.1M of $45M ($2.9M space) with 23 active and 0 taxi — every rookie taken almost certainly goes to taxi (50% cap hit) and we cannot afford more than ~2 active rookies even on minimums.

wildcard.name: Skyler Bell
wildcard.reasoning: Pure 3.06 RSP swing: Consensus has him in 'Free Agents' tier at #33 but RSP #6 DoT 85.9 with a Ja'Marr Chase high-end comp. Medium RSP affinity makes this defensible as a taxi lottery ticket at virtually no cap cost — exactly the asymmetric bet a 23-roster, cap-strapped team should make in round 3.

summary: DREAM is a roster-full, cap-strapped contender drafting around the edges — the goal is to land 1-2 high-conviction taxi stashes (Concepcion at 1.06, Stowers at 1.17 to fix a 2-TE room) and use 2.06/3.06 to chase RSP-vs-Consensus arbitrage (Fields, Skyler Bell). With medium RSP affinity and a 50/25/25 board, the GM won't reach past Consensus Tier 2 on a Waldman fave, but will gladly take a Tier-3 Consensus name when RSP grades him as a top-10 talent.
